content.problemStatement:
Rishabh finds leap year fascinating. He wants to find the next leap year, given the current year. Since Rishabh is a curious kid and asks a lot of questions, you thought to write a program for him.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the year.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the next leap year to the given year.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the given year (a 4‑digit number without leading zeros).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output on a separate line the smallest leap year that is strictly greater than N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2022 → next leap year is 2024. 2000 is a leap year but we need the next one, which is 2004.
2096 is a leap year; the following years 2097‑2099 are not leap years, 2100 is not a leap year (century not divisible by 400), so the next leap year is 2104.
2000 is divisible by 400, therefore it is a leap year and it is the first leap year after 1999.

solution.approach: A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 400 or (divisible by 4 and not divisible by 100). For each input year N we start from year = N + 1 and increase it until the leap‑year condition holds. The gap between two consecutive leap years is at most 8 years, so this simple linear scan is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – at most a few iterations per test case (maximum 8 checks).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
